change_class: ADDITIVE KEYS — two new top-level scalars, no existing field changed
change_note: Adds latest_batch_id (= MAX(batch_id)) and batch_count (= COUNT(*)), both single-derived server-side by getMerkleBatchSummary() in src/lib/performance-db.ts. FIRST snapshot for this endpoint — none existed, which is part of why the defect below shipped unnoticed. Root cause: `batches` is LIMIT-capped at 100 (getMerkleBatches(limit = 100)), and consumers derived BOTH the displayed batch NUMBER and the batch COUNT from batches.length. That equalled the truth only while fewer than 100 batches existed; batch 101 (2026-07-20) crossed the cap and /verify pinned at '#100' permanently while its sibling latest_batch_at span kept updating. All pre-existing fields are BYTE-UNCHANGED. No outcome_* / internal fields — batch metadata is public by construction (it is the on-chain anchor).
source: origin/main src/index.ts /api/merkle-batches builder (res.json) + src/lib/performance-db.ts getMerkleBatchSummary(); pre-deploy capture
error_contract: Unchanged: 500 {error:'Failed to load batches'} on throw. getMerkleBatchSummary() is awaited in the SAME Promise.all as getMerkleBatches(), so a summary failure degrades identically to an existing batches failure — it cannot half-render.
cache_contract: Unchanged. Cloudflare edge Cache Rule (45s) on /api/merkle-batches per system-map annotation; the two new scalars are folded into the same cached body.
consumers: LANDING /verify badge + chip (data-tr-field latest_batch, latest_batch_n, latest_batch_at)
landing/js/track-record-proxy.js (runtime hydration — now reads latest_batch_id / batch_count, with a row-derived fallback for mid-deploy skew)
scripts/snapshot-landing-data.mjs (deploy-time injector; accessor batches.latest.batch_id was ALREADY correct and is unchanged)
drift_check_command: curl -s https://algovault.com/api/merkle-batches | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); ALLOW=['_algovault','batch_count','batches','chain','contractAddress','latest_batch_id']; assert sorted(d.keys())==ALLOW, 'TOP-KEY DRIFT: '+str(sorted(d.keys())); assert d['latest_batch_id']==max(b['batch_id'] for b in d['batches']), 'IDENTITY DRIFT: latest_batch_id != max(batch_id)'; assert d['batch_count']>=len(d['batches']), 'COUNT DRIFT: batch_count < returned rows'; assert d['latest_batch_id']>=d['batch_count'], 'IDENTITY/COUNT INVERSION'; print('shape OK, latest_batch_id', d['latest_batch_id'], 'batch_count', d['batch_count'], 'rows', len(d['batches']))"

anchors_at_capture.note: returned_rows is CAPPED at 100 by design — it is NOT the count and NEVER the batch number. latest_batch_id == batch_count only because batch_ids are contiguous from 1; do not rely on that coincidence. The drift signal is a KEY-set change, latest_batch_id != max(batch_id) over the returned rows, or batch_count < returned_rows.
